feature: Wire hex-analyzer detectors into the sched improver discover phase
description: hex-analyzer already implements four design-quality detectors (god_types, cohesion, composition_churn, duplication) that run via `hex analyze`. The sched improver's discover phase only consumes AdrDoctor today (72 hypotheses per tick, all from one source). Bridging the analyzer outputs into the Hypothesis stream gives the improver real architectural-health signal — the README's claim that the loop 'watches its own design' becomes true rather than aspirational.

tasks.name: Define DetectorFinding -> Hypothesis mapping
tasks.done_condition: fn finding_to_hypothesis(f: DetectorFinding) -> Hypothesis maps each detector kind to a Hypothesis with appropriate severity (god_type=warning, kitchen_sink_port=error, dead_layer=error, composition_churn=info, duplication=warning). source field encodes the detector name.

tasks.name: Stable hypothesis IDs for dedup
tasks.done_condition: fn hypothesis_id(source: &str, scope: &str) -> String returns a content-addressed ID (hash of source+scope+finding-key) so the same finding from successive ticks dedups to one row.

tasks.name: god_types analyzer source
tasks.done_condition: discover.rs::collect_hypotheses() calls hex_analyzer::god_types::scan(repo_root) and folds each finding into a Hypothesis via P1.1. New rows visible with source="GodTypes".
tasks.verify: hex sched improver discover --once includes rows with source=GodTypes after a deliberately-bloated test fixture is added under hex-cli/tests/fixtures/improver/god-type-violation/

tasks.name: cohesion analyzer source
tasks.done_condition: discover.rs calls cohesion::scan(). Findings with cluster_count >= MIN_CLUSTER_COUNT emit Hypothesis source="PortCohesion".
tasks.verify: hex sched improver discover --once includes source=PortCohesion when run against the test corpus

tasks.name: composition_churn analyzer source
tasks.done_condition: discover.rs calls composition_churn::scan(). Findings emit source="CompositionChurn" — flags substrate.composition entries that have changed 3+ times in the past 30 days.
tasks.verify: hex sched improver discover --once includes source=CompositionChurn after seeding a churning composition adapter

tasks.name: duplication analyzer source
tasks.done_condition: discover.rs calls duplication::scan(). Findings with multiset_jaccard >= 0.6 emit source="AdapterDuplication" with both adapter IDs in the scope field.
tasks.verify: hex sched improver discover --once includes source=AdapterDuplication after seeding two adapters with overlapping impl bodies

tasks.name: Per-detector budget cap
tasks.done_condition: detectors.toml gates each detector with [max_findings_per_tick]. Default 10. discover.rs respects the cap. Op env override: HEX_IMPROVER_DETECTOR_BUDGET.

tasks.name: Tick latency SLO
tasks.done_condition: discover() returns within 5s on the hex-intf workspace itself (the most realistic worst case). Each detector wrapped in tokio::time::timeout; on timeout, emits a Hypothesis source="DetectorTimeout" and continues.

tasks.name: Multi-source discover test
tasks.done_condition: Test fixture contains one ADR-doctor finding + one god-type fixture + one cohesion fixture + one duplication fixture. discover --once returns >= 4 hypotheses with distinct source values.

tasks.name: Dashboard: detector breakdown
tasks.done_condition: Improver dashboard view shows hypothesis count grouped by source (bar chart or simple table). Updates in real time via STDB subscription on improver_event.
tasks.verify: dashboard hashroute #/improver shows >= 2 distinct sources after running P4.1
